compiled_state.title: As a developer, ticket link prevents circular and self-referential dependencies at the promoted level
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description
**What**: Add check_cycle_at_level(source_id, target_id, level, tracker_dir) to ticket-graph.py. BFS scoped to tickets whose ticket_type matches the target level (case-insensitive — handles "epic", "Epic", "EPIC"). Detects both cycles and self-loops. Integrate into add_dependency() after resolve_hierarchy_link(): if cycle or self-loop detected, exit non-zero with clear error and write no LINK event. Explicitly coordinate with existing check_would_create_cycle() call in add_dependency().
**Why**: Completes the enforcement chain: hierarchy resolution promotes the link, cycle detection ensures the promoted link is safe.
**Scope**:
- IN: check_cycle_at_level() function, self-loop detection, case-insensitive ticket_type matching (SC12), integration into add_dependency(), explicit coordination with existing check_would_create_cycle()
- OUT: Tests and benchmark (S4)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, attempting ticket link epic-A epic-B depends_on when epic-B already has a path back to epic-A exits non-zero with circular dependency error and writes no LINK event
  ← Satisfies: SC9 (cycle)
- When this story is complete, a cross-epic link whose source and target both resolve to the same epic exits non-zero with self-referential dependency error
  ← Satisfies: SC9 (self-loop)
- When this story is complete, ticket_type values of "Epic", "EPIC", and "epic" in ticket state are all recognized as the epic level for BFS scoping
  ← Satisfies: SC12
- When this story is complete, the existing check_would_create_cycle() behavior is not regressed — all existing callers produce the same results as before
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Architecture] add_dependency() already calls check_would_create_cycle() — explicitly decide whether check_cycle_at_level replaces it for promoted links (preferred: consolidate) or runs alongside it. Document the decision.
- [Performance] BFS must load only same-level tickets — not the full ticket graph. Verify implementation does not accidentally load all tickets.
- [Reliability] Coordinate with check_would_create_cycle() to avoid double-counting or false positives

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
